children.heading: Accredited asbestos contractor
children.label: 1 Accredited asbestos contractor
children.text: The term “accredited asbestos contractor” means a person accredited pursuant to the provisions of .section 2646 of this title

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 2 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency.

children.label: A
children.text: chrysotile (serpentine),

children.label: B
children.text: crocidolite (riebeckite),

children.label: C
children.text: amosite (cummingtonite-grunerite),

children.label: D
children.text: anthophyllite,

children.label: E
children.text: tremolite, or

children.label: F
children.text: actinolite.

children.heading: Asbestos-containing material
children.label: 4 Asbestos-containing material
children.text: The term “asbestos-containing material” means any material which contains more than 1 percent asbestos by weight.

children.heading: EPA guidance document
children.label: 5 EPA guidance document
children.text: The term “Guidance for Controlling Asbestos-Containing Material in Buildings”, means the Environmental Protection Agency document with such title as in effect on .March 31, 1986

children.heading: Friable asbestos-containing material
children.label: 6 Friable asbestos-containing material
children.text: The term “friable asbestos-containing material” means any asbestos-containing material applied on ceilings, walls, structural members, piping, duct work, or any other part of a building which when dry may be crumbled, pulverized, or reduced to powder by hand pressure. The term includes non-friable asbestos-containing material after such previously non-friable material becomes damaged to the extent that when dry it may be crumbled, pulverized, or reduced to powder by hand pressure.

children.label: A
children.text: any local educational agency as defined in ,section 7801 of title 20

children.label: B
children.text: the owner of any private, nonprofit elementary or secondary school building, and

children.label: C
children.text: the governing authority of any school operated under the defense dependents’ education system provided for under the Defense Dependents’ Education Act of 1978 ( et seq.).20 U.S.C. 921

children.heading: Most current guidance document
children.label: 8 Most current guidance document
children.text: The term “most current guidance document” means the Environmental Protection Agency’s “Guidance for Controlling Asbestos-Containing Material in Buildings” as modified by the Environmental Protection Agency after .March 31, 1986

children.heading: Non-profit elementary or secondary school
children.label: 9 Non-profit elementary or secondary school
children.text: The term “non-profit elementary or secondary school” means any elementary school or secondary school (as defined in ) owned and operated by one or more nonprofit corporations or associations no part of the net earnings of which inures, or may lawfully inure, to the benefit of any private shareholder or individual.section 7801 of title 20

children.heading: Public and commercial building
children.label: 10 Public and commercial building
children.text: The term “public and commercial building” means any building which is not a school building, except that the term does not include any residential apartment building of fewer than 10 units.

children.heading: Response action
children.label: 11 Response action
children.text: The term “response action” means methods that protect human health and the environment from asbestos-containing material. Such methods include methods described in chapters 3 and 5 of the Environmental Protection Agency’s “Guidance for Controlling Asbestos-Containing Materials in Buildings”.

children.heading: School
children.label: 12 School
children.text: The term “school” means any elementary school or secondary school as defined in .section 7801 of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: any structure suitable for use as a classroom, including a school facility such as a laboratory, library, school eating facility, or facility used for the preparation of food,

children.label: B
children.text: any gymnasium or other facility which is specially designed for athletic or recreational activities for an academic course in physical education,

children.label: C
children.text: any other facility used for the instruction of students or for the administration of educational or research programs, and

children.label: D
children.text: any maintenance, storage, or utility facility, including any hallway, essential to the operation of any facility described in subparagraphs (A), (B), or (C).

children.heading: State
children.label: 14 State
children.text: The term “State” means a State, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Northern Marianas, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, and the Virgin Islands.
